name: Night Before French Toast
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon Butter or margarine for baking dish
2 tablespoons Butter or margarine - cut into small pieces
10 ounces (about 1 loaf) French bread, sliced 1 inch thick
4 teaspoons Sugar (or sugar equivalent to 4 ts. sugar)
recipeInstructions:
This recipe was posted on P by Maggie Cecil-Zachos - and is a favorite of hers.
1. Grease a 9 x 13 inch glass baking dish with the 1/2 teaspoon butter or margarine. Arrange bread in one layer in baking dish.
2. In a large microwave-safe bowl or pan, mix eggs, salt, sugar, milk, and vanilla. Place the bowl in the microwave and heat on HIGH for five (5) minutes, stirring twice.
3. For heating on the range, place the egg mixture in a medium saucepan and heat on medium, stirring as needed, until hot, but not boiling, about 5 minutes. Be careful not to let the mixture boil, because it will set the eggs.
4. Pour the hot egg mixture gently over the bread slices and cover with plastic wrap. Refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
5. To bake - dot with the 2 tablespoons butter pieces. Place uncovered pan in cold oven. Bake at 350 degrees for 45 to 50 minutes, until bread is puffy and lightly brown. Remove from heat and let stand 5 minutes.
6. Serve with Cary's SF Syrup, sugar-free jelly, or fresh fruit.
Enjoy.
